data.narrative:
Picogrid is a Series A–stage defense and security company founded by Zane Mountcastle, Martin Slosarik, and Dan Chirita that has raised $45M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 10 peers—2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms in the Defense & Security sector at Series A and Series B stages, including Anduril Industries, Helsing, Shield AI, and Saronic.

Two valuation methods triangulate the company's fair value. Stage-progression analysis applies a typical 2.0x step-up multiplier from Series A to the next funding round, using the $45M total raised as a proxy base, yielding $90M. Peer-median analysis examines 8 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables with a median valuation of $1.0B and a range spanning $125M to $3.0B (represented by Dream Security at $3.0B and lower-quartile peers at $125M), producing an implied value of $1.0B.

The triangulated analysis yields a valuation range of $76M (low), $545M (median), to $1.1B (high). The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around revenue trajectory, customer concentration, and the sectoral premium commanded by early-stage defense-tech firms. Stage-progression suggests a more conservative near-term floor, while peer multiples imply significant upside if Picogrid achieves comparable scale and market position to Series B cohorts.
